---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: Go CLI architecture
predicate: follows a pure-core / thin-shell pattern with full dependency injection wired only at main()
object: 'Split into five files: types.go (domain structs + package-level sentinel errors), store.go (Store interface + FileStore + Clock interface + RealClock — no business logic), core.go (App struct with injected store/clock/writer; all business logic; zero os.* or time.Now() calls), display.go (printTable with injected io.Writer and today time.Time for determinism), main.go (DataDir, dispatch map[string]cmdFunc, flag.FlagSet per command, wiring). App is constructed in main() with real impls; tests construct it with MemStore + FakeClock + bytes.Buffer. Sentinel errors are package-level vars returned wrapped with %w; callers use errors.Is. No global mutable state. Stdlib-only; hand-rolled dispatch. This architecture lets every unit of business logic be tested without touching the filesystem or wall clock.'
luhmann: "1"
created: "2026-05-30"
source: distilled L3 generic lesson
---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Go CLI architecture follows a pure-core / thin-shell pattern with full dependency injection wired only at main() Split into five files: types.go (domain structs + package-level sentinel errors), store.go (Store interface + FileStore + Clock interface + RealClock — no business logic), core.go (App struct with injected store/clock/writer; all business logic; zero os.* or time.Now() calls), display.go (printTable with injected io.Writer and today time.Time for determinism), main.go (DataDir, dispatch map[string]cmdFunc, flag.FlagSet per command, wiring). App is constructed in main() with real impls; tests construct it with MemStore + FakeClock + bytes.Buffer. Sentinel errors are package-level vars returned wrapped with %w; callers use errors.Is. No global mutable state. Stdlib-only; hand-rolled dispatch. This architecture lets every unit of business logic be tested without touching the filesystem or wall clock..



**Accumulated (bookmarks):** Accumulated from bookmarks build: the injected dependency set is OPEN-ENDED, not fixed at three. ANY side-effectful operation (file I/O, clock, output, browser-open, network call, notification) becomes a field on the App struct, injected via NewApp(), typed as a minimal func signature (func(arg T) error) rather than a named interface when the collaborator is a single function. Rule: if a function in core.go would call os/exec, net/http, os.Open, or any external-world operation, extract it to an injected func field. Tests override the field directly (app.openURL = stub) or pass a no-op via the constructor; wire the real (often platform-aware) impl only in main().
